sections.name: Overview
content: There are several Color Gradng and Tonemapping controls in Minecraft. Color Grading controls include midtones, highlights, and shadows. Tonemapping operators include Reinhard, Hable, and several other options.
You can override all of these settings to change the contrast, gamma, gain, and more of shadows, midtones, and highlights, as well as how colors are remapped using tonemapping.

sections.name: JSON Format
content: All color grading settings overrides should specify the version that they target via the "format_version" field. The remainder of the color grading data is divided up into independent JSON sub-objects for each control.

These overrides define the data of the overarching color grading settings collection. To be valid, a definition must include exactly one of these type objects. See the full color grading settings schema below for additional details and the full list of supported color grading overrides.
Here is a sample color grading settings file.

sections.name: Adding Color Grading and Tonemapping Settings
sections.id: Adding Color Grading and Tonemapping Settings
content: Color Grading and Tonemapping Settings are read from JSON files in the "color_grading" subfolder of resource packs. Loading enforces one Color Grading and Tonemapping settings collection per file. The settings collection's identifier acts as its name and must be namespaced. If two resource packs define the same Color Grading and Tonemapping settings name (including namespace), then the settings from the highest pack in the stack will be used. Adding a file with a new name to the Color Grading and Tonemapping data location will make it available for the game to use, while existing Color Grading and Tonemapping settings can be overridden using another settings collection with the same identifier.

rows.Name: format_version
rows.Description: Specifies the version of the game these Color Grading Settings were made in. If the version is lower than the current version, any changes made to the settings in the vanilla version will be applied to it.

rows.Name: minecraft:color_grading_settings
rows.Description: Color Grading Settings definition which includes the 'midtones,' 'temperature,' 'shadows,' and 'highlights' sections.

rows.Name: highlights
rows.Description: Parameters to control highlights.

rows.Name: midtones
rows.Description: Parameters to control midtones.

rows.Name: shadows
rows.Description: Parameters to control shadows.

rows.Name: temperature
rows.Description: Parameters to control temperatur.

rows.Name: identifier
rows.Description: The name of this Color Grading Description in the format 'namespace_name:description_name'.

rows.Name: color_grading
rows.Description: Parameters to control color grading.

rows.Name: description
rows.Description: Identifier for the Color Grading Settings.

rows.Name: tone_mapping
rows.Description: Parameters to control tone mapping.

rows.Name: contrast
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which describes the tonal range, the difference in luminance between the bright and dark pixels in an image.

rows.Name: enabled
rows.Description: Whether or not highlights are on or off.

rows.Name: gain
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 10.0 which is a multiplication factor applied to each color channel to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: gamma
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which is an exponential factor applied to the final color after both color grading and tone mapping to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: highlightsMin
rows.Description: A value between 1.0 and 4.0 which is a factor multiplied by the average luminance of the scene to determine which pixels are considered highlights.

rows.Name: offset
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which is an exponential factor applied to the final color after both color grading and tone mapping to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: saturation
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 10.0 which determines the hue intensity of colors.

rows.Name: contrast
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which describes the tonal range, the difference in luminance between the bright and dark pixels in an image.

rows.Name: gain
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 10.0 which is a multiplication factor applied to each color channel to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: gamma
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which is an exponential factor applied to the final color after both color grading and tone mapping to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: offset
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which is an exponential factor applied to the final color after both color grading and tone mapping to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: saturation
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 10.0 which determines the hue intensity of colors.

rows.Name: contrast
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which describes the tonal range, the difference in luminance between the bright and dark pixels in an image.

rows.Name: enabled
rows.Description: Whether or not shadows are on or off.

rows.Name: gain
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 10.0 which is a multiplication factor applied to each color channel to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: gamma
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which is an exponential factor applied to the final color after both color grading and tone mapping to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: offset
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 4.0 which is an exponential factor applied to the final color after both color grading and tone mapping to adjust the overall luminance intensity of the image.

rows.Name: saturation
rows.Description: A value between 0.0 and 10.0 which determines the hue intensity of colors.

rows.Name: shadowsMax
rows.Description: A value between 0.1 and 1.0 which is a factor multiplied by the average luminance of the scene to determine which pixels are considered shadows.

rows.Name: enabled
rows.Description: Whether or not temperature is on or off.

rows.Name: temperature
rows.Description: A value between 1000.0 and 15000.0 which is the overall image temperature measured in Kelvin. The default value is 6500.0, the standard 'daylight' illumination.

rows.Name: type
rows.Description: The name of the desired temperature type from the following list: 'white_balance' or 'color_temperature'.

rows.Name: color_temperature
rows.Description: Causes higher temperature values to result in a warmer image, while lower temperature values will result in a cooler image.

rows.Name: white_balance
rows.Description: Causes higher temperature values to result tin a cooler image, while lower temperature values will result in a warmer image.

rows.Name: operator
rows.Description: The name of the desired tonemapping operator from the following list: 'aces', 'hable', 'reinhard', 'reinhard_luma', 'reinhard_luminance', 'reinhard_luminance', or 'generic'.

rows.Name: aces
rows.Description: A filmic tone mapping operator meant to emulate the behaviors of real-life film, specifically following the ACES standard used in television and film.

rows.Name: generic
rows.Description: A generic tone mapping curve that has been hand-tuned by Mojang's artists. It is similar to the other filmic operators but preserves a bit more hue saturation at high luminance regions.

rows.Name: hable
rows.Description: A filmic tone mapping operator meant to emulate the behaviors of real-life film, developed and shared by John Hable at a GDC talk from 2010 titled  'Uncharted 2 : HDR Lighting'.

rows.Name: reinhard
rows.Description: A low-cost tone mapping operator that remaps HDR color values in the range [0, ∞) to [0, 1). This operator looks especially good when applied to low-contrast scenes whose values are already within the range of [0, 1), but tends to mute higher luminance values.

rows.Name: reinhard_luma
rows.Description: An extended version of the Reinhard operator that comes at a slightly higher cost, but it preserves details in low dynamic range regions of an image that would otherwise be lost by the standard Reinhard operator.

rows.Name: reinhard_luminance
rows.Description: A version of the extended Reinhard operator modified to adjust the luminance of the input colors. It comes at a slightly higher cost, but it preserves colors in high luminance regions of an image that would otherwise be washed out by the standard Reinhard operator.
